claim_for_bridge: The paper says raw executable step is not free-standing Q/B/L but R/S/T macro family on fixed branch.
excerpt: 7.2 Why the old operator-core was not selector-complete The operator-core named the branch maps Q, B, L, but the actual executable kernel step does not fire a free-standing B without phase motion. The exact engine step always advances t and then either continues with balanced refinement, continues without refinement, or lifts host class on carry. The executable step is therefore not {Q, B, L} as three mutually exclusive raw primitives. It is the macro-step family R := Q ◦ B = B ◦ Q, S := Q, T := L

claim_for_bridge: Same-axis saturation is exhaustion of lawful same-axis expression, not discharge or termination.
excerpt: Definition 4.7.1 (Same-axis saturation). The current admitted axis/domain is same-axis saturated when no genuinely new lawful invariant-bearing articulation remains available within that same axis/domain. Any further purported articulation there would either be mere renaming or reparameterization of what is already borne, or would require a new irreducible axis/domain and therefore not belong to the same axis/domain. Clarification. Same-axis saturation is not discharge, not resolution of the invariant, not collapse, not termination, and not a claim that nothing further happens. It is only exhaustion of lawful same-axis expression.

claim_for_bridge: After saturated same-axis expression, terminal discharge excluded, so orthogonal re-articulation is forced.
excerpt: Theorem 4.7.3 (Saturation theorem). Within a given admitted orthogonality class, once lawful same-axis expression of the invariant is exhaustively saturated, further admitted articulation in that same class is impossible without contradiction. Since terminal discharge is excluded, orthogonal re-articulation is forced. Status: Proved in this CF. Proof. By Definition 4.7.1, same-axis saturation means no genuinely new lawful invariant-bearing articulation remains available within the current axis/domain. Suppose nevertheless that a further admitted articulation exists in that same class. If it is genuinely new and lawful, then Definition 4.7.1 was false and the axis/domain was not saturated after all. If it is not genuinely new, then it is only renaming or reparameterization of what is already borne and therefore not a new admitted articulation in that class. Contradiction. Therefore no fur

claim_for_bridge: 2D admission licenses ordered pairs and binary combination, no richer structure claimed.
excerpt: Theorem 4.9.1 (Algebra unlock at 2D). Once 2D is admitted, ordered-pair description and lawful binary combination are licensed. This is the first algebraic unlock of the branch. Status: Proved in this CF. Proof. By Theorem 4.8.3, two irreducible articulation axes/classes are admitted. With two irreducible axes, a determination may be specified by its two-axis placement rather than by a single-axis burden alone. This licenses ordered-pair description (a, b) relative to the admitted pair of axes. Once ordered pairs are licensed, lawful binary combination and comparison of coefficients on those axes are also licensed. That is the first algebraic regime of the branch. No richer algebraic structure is claimed here. □
